scope: Articulate-style original plus translated XLIFF 1.2 file pairs.
decision: Only deterministic metadata-preserving repair classes may emit a repaired XLIFF. Anything that changes translated target text, changes trans-unit count, changes protected inline signatures, changes XLIFF version, or requires semantic judgement is diagnosis-only or rejected.

safeRepairClasses.allowedWhen: A UTF-8 BOM appears before the XML declaration.
The XML declaration advertises a non-UTF-8 encoding while the payload is already readable as text.
Original and translated trans-unit counts match, unit order matches, and only missing or changed translated trans-unit id attributes differ.

categoryPolicy.reason: Leading BOM removal is byte-local and does not affect translated content.
The declaration value can be normalized without touching XML content.
The original id can be restored only when the pair still has matching order and count.
Missing id is repairable only when the translated file still has the same trans-unit count and order. Missing or extra units are unrepairable.
Creating target text is translation work and cannot be inferred safely.
Tag-count drift can corrupt translated content or Articulate protected markup.
Inline tag rewrites must not be auto-corrected without real file proof.
The translated file no longer matches the original XLIFF 1.2 namespace/version contract.
Broken XML must be fixed before structural XLIFF comparison can be trusted.
Unknown entity repair can change text meaning or hide broader XML corruption.
Non-XLIFF XML is outside the product scope.
XLIFF 1.0 and 2.0 require separate diagnosis contracts before support.
Articulate import expects .xlf or .xliff upload files.

blockedUntilProof.requiredProof: Separate fixture corpus and diagnosis-only copy. No repair writer until real Articulate files prove stable signatures.
Separate product promise and fixtures that do not imply safe repair without original file comparison.
Real safely repairable files plus roundtrip tests that preserve target text and Articulate import signatures.
